title: <div>Differentiate a complete sentence from a fragment, run-on, and comma splice
content:
Differentiate a complete sentence from a fragment, run-on, and comma splice. (CO1)
Write well-developed, well-organized paragraphs. (CO1)
Identify agreement and tenses in parts of speech. (CO1)
Recognize dangling modifiers and punctuation such as apostrophes, hyphens, and question marks and their proper usage. (CO1)
Analyze and correct grammatical and mechanical errors in a given passage. (CO1)
Demonstrate ability to Identify the type of audience addressed in an essay. (CO2)
Express an understanding of descriptive writing. (CO2)
Demonstrate the characteristics and organization of narration. (CO3)
Recognize the structure of an essay and its components. (CO3)
Perform prewriting activities to begin the development of a personal narrative. (CO3)
Identify parts of a thesis. (CO4)
Practice creating citations. (CO5)
Identify types of outside sources and the reason to use them. (CO5)
